raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section contains an explicit contribution claim ('first preference-trained image-level router… obviates ground-truth supervision') and an implied gap statement; any review must assess whether these are coherent, scoped, and defensible.","R1.2":"The method rests on a non-trivial logical premise—inter-detector disagreement as a proxy for per-image difficulty—that requires scrutiny of premise validity, the supervised-without-GT inference chain, and coherence across the method/results subsections.","R1.3":"Quantitative claims are specific (53.4 mAP at 38% FLOPs; 71ms vs 184ms latency; 'negligible accuracy loss'); checking that summary language is consistent with reported operating-point numbers is central to reviewing this section.","R1.4":"The section asserts novelty ('first preference-trained image-level router for object detection'); the active prior-work citations (BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet, cascade detectors) must be tested against this delta claim.","R1.6":"'Review this section' without further qualification routinely includes prose assessment; writing polish is treated as central unless the requester specifies a purely technical scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is this a technical/scientific review (prioritizing logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty validation) rather than a writing/editorial review (prioritizing prose clarity and expression quality)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section contains an explicit contribution claim ('first preference-trained image-level router… obviates ground-truth supervision') and an implied gap statement; any review must assess whether these are coherent, scoped, and defensible.
rationale.R1.2: The method rests on a non-trivial logical premise—inter-detector disagreement as a proxy for per-image difficulty—that requires scrutiny of premise validity, the supervised-without-GT inference chain, and coherence across the method/results subsections.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims are specific (53.4 mAP at 38% FLOPs; 71ms vs 184ms latency; 'negligible accuracy loss'); checking that summary language is consistent with reported operating-point numbers is central to reviewing this section.
rationale.R1.4: The section asserts novelty ('first preference-trained image-level router for object detection'); the active prior-work citations (BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet, cascade detectors) must be tested against this delta claim.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' without further qualification routinely includes prose assessment; writing polish is treated as central unless the requester specifies a purely technical scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this a technical/scientific review (prioritizing logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty validation) rather than a writing/editorial review (prioritizing prose clarity and expression quality)?
